summary: Unmerged tooling PR proposing a global context generation workflow for m2c. It would add `tools/generate_context.sh` to generate a whole-codebase `ctx.c` and adjust headers so m2c could process them without errors. The PR was closed after being superseded by PR #462, with no diff available in this slice.

key_files.role: proposed script for generating global m2c context across the entire codebase
key_files.evidence: Mentioned in PR body; no changed-files list or diff was present in the slice.

key_files.role: header files were reportedly modified to avoid m2c errors
key_files.evidence: PR body says header files are modified to not throw errors when run through m2c, but exact files are not available.

what_changed: Proposed creating `tools/generate_context.sh` to generate context for the entire codebase.
Proposed modifying header files so they would not error when processed by m2c.
Documented intended usage: run `tools/generate_context.sh`, then invoke `python tools/m2c/m2c.py -t ppc-mwcc-c --context ctx.c -f func_DEADBABE asm/path/to/file.s`.
Specified that the script should be run from an MSYS2 terminal and that Python must have packages needed by m2c.
The PR was closed unmerged because it was superseded by #462.
smart_moves: Centralizing context generation into one script would reduce repeated manual setup for m2c-based decompilation attempts.
Generating context for the entire codebase aimed to make m2c invocations more reliable by providing broad type, declaration, and macro information.
Calling out MSYS2 and Python package requirements in the PR body helped document the expected local environment for contributors.
Making headers m2c-tolerant recognized that decomp tooling often exercises headers differently from the normal compiler path.
decomp_lessons: Global m2c context is valuable for matching work because it can remove friction when translating assembly functions such as the example `func_DEADBABE`.
Headers used by normal builds may still need compatibility adjustments for analysis tools like m2c.
Tooling PRs should include clear invocation examples and environment assumptions, especially when they depend on MSYS2 and Python packages.
Because this PR was superseded and no diff is present, future work should inspect PR #462 for the accepted implementation details before reusing this approach.

naming_conventions.name: tools/generate_context.sh
naming_conventions.lesson: Use descriptive snake_case shell script names under `tools/` for contributor-facing decomp utilities.

naming_conventions.name: ctx.c
naming_conventions.lesson: The PR body used a short conventional filename for m2c context passed via `--context`.

naming_conventions.name: func_DEADBABE
naming_conventions.lesson: The usage example follows the common placeholder form for an address-derived unnamed function.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use m2c with the Metrowerks PowerPC C target: `-t ppc-mwcc-c`.
Pass generated whole-codebase context to m2c with `--context ctx.c`.
Target a specific assembly function with `-f func_DEADBABE asm/path/to/file.s`.
Prepare headers so m2c can parse them cleanly before relying on generated context for matching work.

review_feedback.impact: This PR was closed unmerged; the accepted or newer global context generation approach should be looked up in PR #462.
